revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=白鳥塚古墳 |所在地=三重県鈴鹿市上田町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|scale:5000}} |形状=帆立貝式古墳 |規模=墳長80m、高さ13m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=円筒埴輪 |史跡指定=[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）三重県指定 |画像= }}
'''白鳥塚古墳'''（しらとりづかこふん）は、[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある、7基から成る白鳥塚古墳群の主墳。
正式名称は'''白鳥塚古墳1号墳'''で、三重県下最大の帆立貝式古墳である。
== 概要 == *[[ヤマトタケル]]の白鳥伝説から、白鳥塚と呼ばれた。異名に、ヒヨドリ塚、茶臼山、丸山など。 *[[本居宣長]]<ref>『[[古事記伝]]』（本居宣長）は、白鳥塚を能褒野墓の第一候補としている。</ref>や[[平田篤胤]]ら[[江戸時代]]後期の[[国学者]]の多くが、白鳥塚を[[古事記]]にある[[ヤマトタケル]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓として最有力視した。 *[[明治]]9年（[[1976年]]）[[教部省]]は白鳥塚をヤマトタケル陵墓と治定したが、明治12年（[[1879年]]）に[[宮内省]]はそれを覆し、丁子塚（現亀山市）を治定した。以後、丁子塚がヤマトタケル陵墓（[[能褒野王塚古墳]]）とされた。 *[[平成]]16年から17年にかけての学術調査で、古来[[円墳]]とされていた形状が、帆立貝式古墳であることが確認された。
===構造=== *墳長80メートル *後円部墳径64メートル、墳丘高9メートル *前方部墳長16メートル、幅27メートル *最下部に基壇を設け、その上の墳丘は2段構造となっている。
=== 副葬品 === 中世以降の戦乱による荒廃もあり、平成16年から17年にかけての学実調査で複数の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているのみである。 明治初期、石室が出土したと伝わるが、確認されていない。
==所在地・交通== *三重県鈴鹿市上田町 **[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分
==周辺情報== *[[加佐登神社]] - ヤマトタケルノミコトを祭神とする。
==脚注・参考文献== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/> *『鈴鹿市史』（鈴鹿市編纂）
== 関連項目 == * [[能褒野王塚古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf 白鳥塚古墳発掘調査資料]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりつかこふん}} [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:三重県の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=白鳥塚古墳 |所在地=三重県鈴鹿市上田町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|scale:5000}} |形状=帆立貝式古墳 |規模=墳長80m、高さ13m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=円筒埴輪 |史跡指定=[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）三重県指定 |画像= }}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=白鳥塚古墳 |所在地=三重県鈴鹿市上田町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|scale:5000}} |形状=帆立貝式古墳 |規模=墳長80m、高さ13m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=円筒埴輪 |史跡指定=[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）三重県指定<ref>{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166 |title=白鳥塚古墳 |publisher=三重県教育委員会 |accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |画像=[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|260px]] }} [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|260px|thumb|参道入口]] '''白鳥塚古墳'''（しらとりづかこふん）は、[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある、7基から成る白鳥塚古墳群の主墳。
正式名称は'''白鳥塚古墳1号墳'''で、三重県下最大の帆立貝式古墳である。
== 概要 == *[[ヤマトタケル]]の白鳥伝説から、白鳥塚と呼ばれた。異名に、ヒヨドリ塚、茶臼山、丸山など。 *[[本居宣長]]<ref>『[[古事記伝]]』（本居宣長）は、白鳥塚を能褒野墓の第一候補としている。</ref>や[[平田篤胤]]ら[[江戸時代]]後期の[[国学者]]の多くが、白鳥塚を[[古事記]]にある[[ヤマトタケル]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓として最有力視した。 *[[明治]]9年（[[1976年]]）[[教部省]]は白鳥塚をヤマトタケル陵墓と治定したが、明治12年（[[1879年]]）に[[宮内省]]はそれを覆し、丁子塚（現亀山市）を治定した。以後、丁子塚がヤマトタケル陵墓（[[能褒野王塚古墳]]）とされた。 *[[平成]]16年から17年にかけての学術調査で、古来[[円墳]]とされていた形状が、帆立貝式古墳であることが確認された。
===構造=== *墳長78メートル *後円部墳径67メートル、墳丘高9メートル *前方部墳長16.4メートル、幅27メートル *最下部に基壇を設け、その上の墳丘は2段構造となっている。 *型式から、5世紀前半に築造されたとみられる。
=== 副葬品 === 中世以降の戦乱による荒廃もあり、平成16年から17年にかけての学実調査で複数の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているのみである。 明治初期、石室が出土したと伝わるが、確認されていない。
==所在地・交通== *三重県鈴鹿市上田町 **[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分
==周辺情報== *[[加佐登神社]] - ヤマトタケルノミコトを祭神とする。
==脚注・参考文献== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/> *『鈴鹿市史』（鈴鹿市編纂）
== 関連項目 == * [[能褒野王塚古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf 白鳥塚古墳発掘調査資料]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりつかこふん}} [[Category:三重県指定史跡]] [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:鈴鹿市]]

==所在地・交通== *三重県鈴鹿市上田町 **[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分
==周辺情報== *[[加佐登神社]] - ヤマトタケルノミコトを祭神とする。

===構造=== *墳長78メートル *後円部墳径67メートル、墳丘高9メートル *前方部墳長16.4メートル、幅27メートル *最下部に基壇を設け、その上の墳丘は2段構造となっている。 *型式から、5世紀前半に築造されたとみられる。
=== 副葬品 === 中世以降の戦乱による荒廃もあり、平成16年から17年にかけての学実調査で複数の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているのみである。 明治初期、石室が出土したと伝わるが、確認されていない。
==所在地・交通== *三重県鈴鹿市上田町 **[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分

revision.text:
{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |Coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |Image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== Location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]
[[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suzuka, Mie]] [[Category:Mie Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend|Tomb Shiratorizuka Kofun]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳]]

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== Location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound |name=Shiratorizuka Kofun |location={{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |shape=Scallop-shaped kofun |size=Length: 80m, Height: 13m |era=Early 5th century |artifacts=Cylindrical haniwa |designation=Mie Prefectural Historic Site (designated in 1937) |image=[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Entrance to the shrine approach]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, consisting of seven burial mounds, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture. Officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''', it is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in the prefecture.
== Overview ==
The tumulus derives its name, Shiratorizuka ("White Bird Mound"), from the legend of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. It is also known by other names, such as Hiyodoritsuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
During the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, many {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学者}} scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>In his work {{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}, Motoori Norinaga identified Shiratorizuka as the primary candidate for the location of the Nobono Tomb of Yamato Takeru mentioned in the Kojiki.</ref> and Hirata Atsutane, considered the mound the most likely location of the Nobono tomb (能褒野陵墓) described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religious Education|ja|教部省}} designated Shiratorizuka as the burial site of Yamato Takeru. However, in 1879, the {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|宮内省}} overturned this decision, designating Chōjizuka (modern-day {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as the official tomb, now identified as the {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A 2004–2005 academic survey revealed that, contrary to its historical designation as a circular mound (円墳), Shiratorizuka is actually a scallop-shaped kofun.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Diameter of the posterior circular mound: 67 meters; height: 9 meters * Length of the anterior rectangular portion: 16.4 meters; width: 27 meters * The mound has a two-tiered structure built atop a foundational platform. * Based on its construction style, it is believed to date to the early 5th century.
== Grave Goods ==
Due to damage from medieval warfare, few artifacts have been recovered. However, the 2004–2005 survey unearthed several {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}. There are reports of a stone burial chamber discovered in the early Meiji period, but its existence has not been confirmed.
== Location ==
* Uedachō, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kisato Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Kisato Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} – dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "History of Suzuka City," compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline}} * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Excavation report on Shiratorizuka Kofun (PDF)]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}

Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.

A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Infobox ancient site |name=Shiratorizuka Kofun |location={{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |shape=Scallop-shaped kofun |size=Length: 80m, Height: 13m |era=Early 5th century |artifacts=Cylindrical haniwa |designation=Mie Prefectural Historic Site (designated in 1937) |image=[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Entrance to the shrine approach]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, consisting of seven burial mounds, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture. Officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''', it is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in the prefecture.
== Overview ==
The tumulus derives its name, Shiratorizuka ("White Bird Mound"), from the legend of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. It is also known by other names, such as Hiyodoritsuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
During the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, many {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学者}} scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>In his work {{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}, Motoori Norinaga identified Shiratorizuka as the primary candidate for the location of the Nobono Tomb of Yamato Takeru mentioned in the Kojiki.</ref> and Hirata Atsutane, considered the mound the most likely location of the Nobono tomb (能褒野陵墓) described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religious Education|ja|教部省}} designated Shiratorizuka as the burial site of Yamato Takeru. However, in 1879, the {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|宮内省}} overturned this decision, designating Chōjizuka (modern-day {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as the official tomb, now identified as the {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A 2004–2005 academic survey revealed that, contrary to its historical designation as a circular mound (円墳), Shiratorizuka is actually a scallop-shaped kofun.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Diameter of the posterior circular mound: 67 meters; height: 9 meters * Length of the anterior rectangular portion: 16.4 meters; width: 27 meters * The mound has a two-tiered structure built atop a foundational platform. * Based on its construction style, it is believed to date to the early 5th century.
== Grave Goods ==
Due to damage from medieval warfare, few artifacts have been recovered. However, the 2004–2005 survey unearthed several {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}. There are reports of a stone burial chamber discovered in the early Meiji period, but its existence has not been confirmed.
== Location ==
* Uedachō, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kisato Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Kisato Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} – dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "History of Suzuka City," compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline}} * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Excavation report on Shiratorizuka Kofun (PDF)]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Infobox ancient site |name=Shiratorizuka Kofun |location={{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |shape=Scallop-shaped kofun |size=Length: 80m, Height: 13m |era=Early 5th century |artifacts=Cylindrical haniwa |designation=Mie Prefectural Historic Site (designated in 1937) |image=[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Entrance to the shrine approach]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, consisting of seven burial mounds, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|ja|鈴鹿市}}, Mie Prefecture. Officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''', it is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in the prefecture.
== Overview ==
The tumulus derives its name, Shiratorizuka ("White Bird Mound"), from the legend of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. It is also known by other names, such as Hiyodoritsuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
During the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, many {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学者}} scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>In his work {{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}, Motoori Norinaga identified Shiratorizuka as the primary candidate for the location of the Nobono Tomb of Yamato Takeru mentioned in the Kojiki.</ref> and Hirata Atsutane, considered the mound the most likely location of the Nobono tomb (能褒野陵墓) described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In 1876, the {{ill|Ministry of Religious Education|ja|教部省}} designated Shiratorizuka as the burial site of Yamato Takeru. However, in 1879, the {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|宮内省}} overturned this decision, designating Chōjizuka (modern-day {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as the official tomb, now identified as the {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A 2004–2005 academic survey revealed that, contrary to its historical designation as a circular mound (円墳), Shiratorizuka is actually a scallop-shaped kofun.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Diameter of the posterior circular mound: 67 meters; height: 9 meters * Length of the anterior rectangular portion: 16.4 meters; width: 27 meters * The mound has a two-tiered structure built atop a foundational platform. * Based on its construction style, it is believed to date to the early 5th century.
== Grave Goods ==
Due to damage from medieval warfare, few artifacts have been recovered. However, the 2004–2005 survey unearthed several {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}. There are reports of a stone burial chamber discovered in the early Meiji period, but its existence has not been confirmed.
== Location ==
* Uedachō, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kisato Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Kisato Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} – dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "History of Suzuka City," compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Nobono Ōzuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == {{commonscat-inline}} * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Excavation report on Shiratorizuka Kofun (PDF)]

== Location ==
* Uedachō, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture

* Approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kisato Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.

* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=qq&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
