'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=qq&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳]] [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:hiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |Artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa |Historic Designation = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (designated in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.

The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.

In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-typed kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-typed.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Artifacts ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs designated Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead designated {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base platform. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only artifacts confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture other_designation (other_designation1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture other_designation (other_designation1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |Era = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture other_designation (other_designation1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== Structure ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture other_designation (other_designation1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture other_designation (other_designation1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs other_designation1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead other_designation1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.

In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.

* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|12=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|12=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|12=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
