[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|12=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|12=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|12=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|12=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|12=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|12=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|12=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|12=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|12=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|12=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=白鳥塚古墳 |所在地=[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]上田町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |形状=帆立貝式古墳 |規模=墳長80m、高さ13m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=円筒埴輪 |史跡指定=[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）三重県指定<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166 |title=白鳥塚古墳 |publisher=三重県教育委員会 |accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |画像=[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|参道入口]]
'''白鳥塚古墳'''（しらとりづかこふん）は、[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある、7基から成る白鳥塚古墳群の主墳。
正式名称は'''白鳥塚古墳1号墳'''で、三重県下最大の帆立貝式古墳である。
== 概要 == [[ヤマトタケル]]の白鳥伝説から、白鳥塚と呼ばれた。異名に、ヒヨドリ塚、茶臼山、丸山など。
[[本居宣長]]<ref>『[[古事記伝]]』（本居宣長）は、白鳥塚を能褒野墓の第一候補としている。</ref>や[[平田篤胤]]ら[[江戸時代]]後期の[[国学者]]の多くが、白鳥塚を[[古事記]]にある[[ヤマトタケル]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓として最有力視した。
[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）[[教部省]]は白鳥塚をヤマトタケル陵墓と治定したが、明治12年（[[1879年]]）に[[宮内省]]はそれを覆し、丁子塚（現在の[[亀山市]]）を治定した。以後、丁子塚がヤマトタケル陵墓（[[能褒野王塚古墳]]）とされた。
[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）から17年（[[2005年]]）にかけての学術調査で、古来[[円墳]]とされていた形状が、帆立貝式古墳であることが確認された。
== 構造 == * 墳長78メートル * 後円部墳径67メートル、墳丘高9メートル * 前方部墳長16.4メートル、幅27メートル * 最下部に基壇を設け、その上の墳丘は2段構造となっている。 * 型式から、5世紀前半に築造されたとみられる。
== 副葬品 == 中世以降の戦乱による荒廃もあり、平成16年から17年にかけての学実調査で複数の[[円筒埴輪]]が出土しているのみである。 明治初期、石室が出土したと伝わるが、確認されていない。
== 所在地 == * 三重県鈴鹿市上田町
== 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[関西線 (名古屋地区)|関西本線]] [[加佐登駅]]下車、徒歩で約30分。
== 周辺情報 == * [[加佐登神社]] - ヤマトタケルノミコトを祭神とする。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== 参考文献 == * 『鈴鹿市史』（鈴鹿市編纂）
== 関連項目 == * [[能褒野王塚古墳]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline}} * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf 白鳥塚古墳発掘調査資料]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりつかこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県指定史跡]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓しらとりつかこふん]]

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|参道入口]]

== 外部リンク == {{commonscat-inline}} * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf 白鳥塚古墳発掘調査資料]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka|qq=}} Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojikiden|ja|古事記伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojikiden|qq=}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Atsutane|qq=}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}.
In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameyama, Mie|qq=}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka|ja|鈴鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka|qq=}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|ja|加佐登駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].

However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|05|N|136|31|34|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_map_size:5000|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|qid=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|qq=|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.

The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
