However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] kokugaku scholars, including [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]]<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojikiden]]'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]]) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]] Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojikiden}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiratorizuka kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojikiden}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojikiden|1=Kojiki-den}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.edu.city.suzuka.mie.jp/museum/shiratori-gen.pdf Shiratorizuka Kofun Excavation Data]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shiratorizuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.901389|N|136.526111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |area = Total length 80m, height 13m |built = Early 5th century |burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa |alternate_name = Mie Prefecture Historic Site (Historic Site1 in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 12 (1937))<ref>{{Cite web|title=白鳥塚古墳|url=http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/item/detail.asp?spetype=11&prePageNum=2&mngnum=0000166|publisher=Mie Prefectural Board of Education|accessdate=2012-08-05}}</ref> |image = [[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun.JPG|250px]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }}
[[File:Shiratoriduka Kohun2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Pathway Entrance]]
'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojikiden|1=Kojiki-den}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
== location ==
* {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}} Ueda-cho, Mie Prefecture
== Access ==
* Approximately 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西線 (名古屋地区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656377}} operated by JR.
== Nearby Sites ==
* {{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine dedicated to Yamato Takeru no Mikoto.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}}
== Sources == * ''Suzuka City History'' (鈴鹿市史), compiled by Suzuka City.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}

'''Shiratorizuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳) is the main tumulus of the Shiratorizuka Kofun Group, which consists of seven tumuli, located in {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Shiratorizuka Kofun No. 1''' and is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Mie Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The kofun derives its name, "Shiratorizuka," from the {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'s White Bird Legend. It is also known by various names such as Hiyodorizuka, Chausuyama, and Maruyama.
Many late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} kokugaku scholars, including {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}<ref>Motoori Norinaga's ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojikiden|1=Kojiki-den}}'' identified Shiratorizuka as the most likely candidate for the Nobono tomb described in the ''Kojiki''.</ref> and {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, regarded Shiratorizuka as the most plausible site for the Nobono Tomb (能褒野陵墓) of Yamato Takeru as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.
In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9 (1876), the Ministry of Religious Affairs Historic Site1 Shiratorizuka as Yamato Takeru's tomb. However, this decision was overturned by the Imperial Household Ministry in Meiji 12 (1879), which instead Historic Site1 {{ill|Chōjizuka|ja|丁子塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjizuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}) as Yamato Takeru's tomb, currently recognized as the {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}.
A scholarly survey conducted between {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 16 (2004) and 17 (2005) confirmed that Shiratorizuka, historically considered a circular kofun, is actually scallop-shaped.
== burial ==
* Total length: 78 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 67 meters, height: 9 meters * Front rectangular mound length: 16.4 meters, width: 27 meters * The mound features a two-tiered structure with a base plattype. * Based on its design, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== burial goods ==
Due to degradation caused by medieval conflicts, the only burial goods confirmed during the academic survey from Heisei 16 to 17 were multiple cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. Reports from the early Meiji period suggest the discovery of a stone chamber, though its existence has not been verified.
